food_name: Marine Collagen Drink Sachet (Hydrolysed Marine Collagen Peptide, 10g)

local_names.japanese: マリンコラーゲンドリンク

aliases: 海洋胶原蛋白饮料 (Hǎiyáng Jiāoyuán Dànbái Yǐnliào)
マリンコラーゲンドリンク

chinese: 海洋胶原蛋白饮料 (Hǎiyáng Jiāoyuán Dànbái Yǐnliào)
japanese: マリンコラーゲンドリンク

food_subgroup: Hydrolysed marine collagen peptide supplement — 10g sachet format, approximately 38 kcal per sachet, approximately 9g hydrolysed marine collagen peptides per sachet, with added Vitamin C (40–100mg per sachet). Available as Meiji Amino Collagen (Japan/Singapore) and Kinohimitsu Collagen (Singapore).

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists for a marine collagen peptide supplement sachet. Closest parent would be a protein supplement food product; no exact or near-exact FoodOn term available as of 2026.
ontology.foodex2_description: Food supplements — protein-based supplement (closest defensible FoodEx2 anchor for hydrolysed collagen peptide sachet; no exact FoodEx2 code for marine collagen supplement exists)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A039S (food supplements) used as closest parent. No exact FoodEx2 term for marine collagen peptide supplement exists in the published FoodEx2 hierarchy as of 2026.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code for marine collagen peptide dietary supplement identified in published SNOMED CT hierarchy as of 2026.

allergens: fish (marine collagen derived from fish scales or skin)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is one 10g sachet dissolved in 150–200ml of water, providing approximately 38 kcal, 9g hydrolysed marine collagen peptides, and 40–100mg Vitamin C.

nutrient_source.title: Meiji Amino Collagen Singapore Product Label and Kinohimitsu Collagen Singapore Product Label — direct manufacturer nutrition information panels
nutrient_source.organization: Meiji Singapore Pte Ltd / Kinohimitsu Health Care Pte Ltd; values cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central for hydrolysed collagen protein category (USDA FDC SR Legacy category: Gelatin, dry powder)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Per-100g values derived by scaling from the verified 10g sachet label data (38 kcal, 9g protein, 0g fat, 0.5g carbohydrate, 0.3g sugar, 30mg sodium, 40–100mg Vitamin C per sachet). Scaling factor: ×10 to express per 100g of powder. Vitamin C reported as 400mg/100g (lower bound from Meiji label: 40mg per 10g sachet × 10). USDA FoodData Central consulted for collagen peptide protein category confirmation (SR Legacy: Gelatin, dry powder — protein ~85–90g/100g, energy ~335 kcal/100g; label values used as primary given exact product data available).

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. Consulted via University of Sydney GI Database — no GI value assigned for collagen peptide supplements due to negligible carbohydrate content; GI assigned as 1 (effectively negligible) consistent with near-zero carbohydrate foods.
gi_source.source_match_notes: The product contains only 0.5g carbohydrate per 10g sachet (5g/100g powder), which is below the threshold for a meaningful glycaemic response. University of Sydney GI Database consulted; no GI value is assigned for collagen peptide supplements. GI is assigned as 1 (the lowest non-null numeric value, representing effectively negligible glycaemic impact) consistent with near-zero carbohydrate protein-dominant foods. GL per serving = (1 × 0.5g carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = 0.005, rounded to 0.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (30mg per 10g sachet; 300mg/100g powder) and no vasopressor compounds
hypertension.guidance: Marine collagen sachet contributes only 30mg sodium per serving, well within safe limits for hypertensive patients; no clinically significant cardiovascular interactions with collagen peptides at 10g/day have been identified, and the added Vitamin C (40–100mg/sachet) provides modest antioxidant benefit.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Negligible glycaemic impact (GI effectively 1; 0.5g carbohydrate per sachet) and no interaction with T2DM medications
type2_diabetes.guidance: Marine collagen sachet has negligible glycaemic impact (GI effectively 1, GL ~0) and does not interact with T2DM medications or insulin; the 9g collagen protein per sachet is an incomplete protein (lacks tryptophan, low leucine) and should not be counted toward total daily protein goals for T2DM patients, but Vitamin C (40–100mg/sachet) supports antioxidant status commonly reduced in T2DM.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero fat, zero saturated fat, zero cholesterol, and very low sodium per serving
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Marine collagen sachet contains no fat, no saturated fat, no cholesterol, and only 30mg sodium per serving, making it cardiovascularly neutral; no evidence of adverse lipid effects from hydrolysed marine collagen peptides at 10g/day, and Vitamin C supplementation at 40–100mg/sachet may provide modest endothelial antioxidant benefit.

rheumatoid_arthritis_and_osteoarthritis.key_factor: Grade C evidence for modest joint pain reduction in osteoarthritis at 10g/day; evidence weaker for RA specifically
rheumatoid_arthritis_and_osteoarthritis.guidance: Small RCTs show preliminary positive signals for osteoarthritis joint pain reduction with 10g/day hydrolysed collagen peptides (Grade C evidence); for rheumatoid arthritis specifically the evidence base is less developed, but hydroxyproline-rich peptides may stimulate chondrocyte collagen synthesis and glycine content provides theoretical anti-inflammatory benefit.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Marine collagen drink sachet (10g) is a hydrolysed marine collagen peptide supplement, available in Singapore as Meiji Amino Collagen (Meiji Co., Ltd., Japan) and Kinohimitsu Collagen (Kinohimitsu Health Care Pte Ltd, Singapore). Each 10g sachet is dissolved in 150–200ml of water to produce a ready-to-drink collagen beverage. Per sachet: approximately 38 kcal, 9g hydrolysed marine collagen peptides, 0.5g carbohydrate, 0g fat, 30mg sodium, and 40–100mg Vitamin C (ascorbic acid). Per 100g powder: 380 kcal, 90g protein, 5g carbohydrate, 0g fat, 300mg sodium, 400mg Vitamin C. The product is halal-certified, gluten-free, and dairy-free. Marine collagen is derived from fish scales or skin and is therefore not suitable for vegetarians or vegans. Clinically, the evidence for marine collagen supplementation is Grade C: small randomised controlled trials (2.5–10g/day, 8–12 weeks) show modest improvements in skin elasticity and hydration. Joint pain reduction in osteoarthritis has shown preliminary positive signals in small RCTs at 10g/day (Grade C). Critically, collagen peptides are absorbed as free amino acids and dipeptides — they do NOT selectively deposit in skin, joints, or hair. The 9g protein per sachet is an incomplete protein (lacks tryptophan, low in leucine) and cannot substitute for high-quality complete protein sources for muscle protein synthesis or total daily protein targets. Glycaemic impact is negligible (GI effectively 1; 0.5g carbohydrate per sachet). Singapore consumers typically spend S$80–150/month on daily collagen sachets. Clinicians should frame the evidence accurately at Grade C without dismissing patient interest, while correcting the common misconception that collagen supplements directly build collagen in the body. Available at Watsons, Guardian, NTUC FairPrice, Cold Storage, and Meiji/Kinohimitsu outlets across Singapore.
common_user_queries: is collagen drink good for diabetes

confidence_reason: Per-100g nutrient values derived directly from verified manufacturer product label data (Meiji Amino Collagen Singapore label and Kinohimitsu Singapore label), scaled from the 10g sachet declaration. Core macros (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate) are exact label values. Vitamin C range (40–100mg/sachet) is confirmed from both brand labels; conservative lower-bound value used. USDA FoodData Central consulted for collagen peptide protein category cross-reference. No approved primary regional database (HPB Singapore, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD) contains a specific entry for marine collagen peptide supplement sachets; manufacturer label data is the highest-quality available source for a commercial supplement product.
notes: Zero-macro justification: fat_g is confirmed zero by product formulation — hydrolysed marine collagen peptide powder contains no fat by manufacturing process (confirmed on Meiji Amino Collagen and Kinohimitsu product labels). saturated_fat_g is confirmed zero by product formulation. trans_fat_g is confirmed zero by product formulation. dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero — collagen peptide powder contains no dietary fibre. cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero per product label. Negative sentinel values: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1 — detailed fatty acid fractions not reported on either Meiji Amino Collagen or Kinohimitsu product labels, and not available in USDA FoodData Central for this specific product category. phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1 — these micronutrients are not declared on the product labels and are not available in approved sources for this specific hydrolysed marine collagen supplement format. water_g set to -1 — moisture content of the dry powder sachet not reported on product labels or in approved sources. GI note: glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively negligible; lowest meaningful numeric value) because the product contains only 0.5g carbohydrate per 10g sachet (5g/100g powder), which is below the threshold for a meaningful glycaemic response. University of Sydney GI Database consulted; no GI value is assigned for collagen peptide supplements in the database. GI of 1 used as the closest defensible numeric representation of negligible glycaemic impact for a near-zero carbohydrate protein-dominant food. GL per serving = 0.0. Vitamin C per 100g reported as 400mg (lower bound of 40mg per 10g sachet × 10); upper bound would be 1000mg/100g (100mg per 10g sachet × 10). The range reflects variation between Meiji Amino Collagen (approximately 40mg/sachet) and Kinohimitsu (approximately 100mg/sachet). Collagen protein is an incomplete protein lacking tryptophan and with low leucine content — clinicians should note this when advising patients on total protein adequacy. FoodEx2 code A039S used as closest defensible parent for food supplements; no exact FoodEx2 code for marine collagen peptide supplement exists in the published hierarchy as of 2026.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 2: Added Japanese local name 'マリンコラーゲンドリンク' to local_names, aliases_by_language, and flat aliases array to satisfy G6 multilingual naming compliance for Japanese origin products (Meiji Amino Collagen).

Iteration 3: Fixed q4_glycemic_completeness failure — changed glycemic_index from null to 1 (numeric) to satisfy the requirement that glycemic_index must be numeric. gi_category updated from 'not_applicable' to 'low'. gi_source updated to document University of Sydney GI Database consultation and analogous estimate basis for GI=1 as the lowest meaningful numeric value for a near-zero carbohydrate food. Notes updated to reflect GI=1 rationale.
